arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line objdump 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which dotnet 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which symchk 2>/dev/null || true

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try the ELF debug info file
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/_.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -5

# Also try downloading the binary itself with symbols
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/${FILENAME} \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -5
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this Android tombstone. The key library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at offsets:

- #01 pc 0x222098
- #02 pc 0x2241ac
- #03 pc 0x1abc80
- #04 pc 0x1abd14
- #05 pc 0x16dfa4
- #06 pc 0x16db6c


- #07 pc 0x16de04

To resolve these addresses to function names, I need to fetch the debug symbols for this specific build from Microsoft's symbol server and then use a tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to map each offset to its corresponding symbol.

The Microsoft symbol server has different URL patterns depending on the binary format. For ELF files like this, I can try constructing the URL with the BuildId and filename, or use the `dotnet-symbol` tool which handles this automatically. so I'll construct the proper URLs for both the binary and debug symbols on the Microsoft symbol server, then attempt to fetch them to see if this particular BuildId actually exists in their repository. I should also check what debugging tools are available locally like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` that could help analyze the symbols once retrieved.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line objdump 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which dotnet 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which symchk 2>/dev/null || true

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try the ELF debug info file
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/_.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -5

# Also try downloading the binary itself with symbols
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/${FILENAME} \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  -w "\nHTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1 | tail -5
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
